name: Zuppa Di Asparagi (Calabrian Asparagus Soup)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Asparagus; trimmed & cut into 1-inch pieces
4 cups Vegetable broth; OR 4 cups Low-sodium chicken broth
1/2 cup Parmesan; grated, plus extra for serving
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, cook the garlic in the olive oil over moderate heat, stirring constantly, until golden, about 3 minutes.
2. Add the asparagus and cook until bright green, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the salt, pepper, and Vegetable Broth (or chicken broth) to the pan. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce the heat to moderately low and simmer until the asparagus is tender, about 15 minutes.
5. In a small bowl, beat the eggs with half of the Parmesan cheese.
6. Reduce the heat to low so that the soup is no longer simmering. Slowly ladle about 2 cups of hot soup into the beaten eggs, whisking constantly.
7. Once incorporated, gradually stir the egg mixture into the soup in the saucepan. Increase the heat to moderately low and cook the soup, whisking constantly, until it just thickens, about 8 minutes. Do not allow the soup to boil or the eggs will curdle.
8. Remove from the heat. Place one slice of toasted bread in each soup bowl. Ladle the hot soup into the bowls and serve with additional grated Parmesan.
Note: Thickening soup with bread is typical of rustic Italian cooking, where nothing goes to waste. The toasts may be made from day-old or slightly stale bread. Serve with a Sauvignon Blanc.
